name: Kathie Jenkins Wild Rice Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add celery, carrots, all onions, and almonds. Sauté until slightly tender, stirring occasionally for about 5 minutes.
2. Add dill weed, black pepper, garlic salt, bay leaves, and turmeric to the skillet. Stir to combine.
3. In a separate large pot, bring chicken stock, wild rice, white rice, and salt to a boil. Reduce heat and add the celery mixture. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes. If the soup becomes too thick, add more stock as needed.
4. Whisk 1 cup of hot soup into the egg yolks. Then whisk this back into the soup.
5. Add cooked chicken and mushrooms to the pot. Discard bay leaves. Gently heat the soup until warmed through; do not boil.
6. Serve immediately. Each serving contains about 282 calories, 1,444 mg sodium, 111 mg cholesterol, 12 grams fat, 28 grams carbohydrates, 16 grams protein, and .84 grams fiber.
